title: Fix Daily Page date selection to use Indian Standard Time (IST, UTC+5:30)

requirements.summary: Fix 'Mark all travellers for today' checkbox on Daily Page to use IST (UTC+5:30) instead of UTC for determining the current date
acceptanceCriteria: When the 'Mark all travellers for today' checkbox is clicked, it marks trips for the current calendar date in IST (UTC+5:30), not UTC.
At any time of day in India, the checkbox selects the correct local date and does not jump to the next day.
No other Daily Page functionality is broken by this change.
The IST date derivation is consistent with any shared date utility (dateRange.ts) — refactor to a shared IST 'today' helper if one does not already exist.

file_operations.description: Add a reusable getTodayIST() utility function that returns the current date string (YYYY-MM-DD format) in IST (UTC+5:30) by applying a +19800 second offset to the current UTC time. This function will be used consistently across the app to derive 'today' for display and default values. The implementation must hardcode the IST offset and not rely on the device timezone.

Update the 'Mark all travellers for today (AM & PM)' checkbox logic to use the new getTodayIST() utility function from dateRange.ts instead of any existing UTC-based date derivation (e.g., new Date()). Ensure that when the checkbox is clicked, it marks trips for the correct IST date and does not select a day ahead. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Audit and fix all other usages of date derivation across the frontend to use IST (UTC+5:30) for today's date defaults
acceptanceCriteria: All date inputs that default to 'today' across the app show the correct IST date.
A single reusable IST 'today' utility function (e.g., getTodayIST()) is used consistently so timezone logic is not duplicated.
No form or page shows a date that is one day ahead of the actual IST date.

file_operations.description: Replace any current date derivation logic used for the default date field with a call to getTodayIST() from dateRange.ts to ensure the form defaults to the correct IST date when opened.

Replace any current date derivation logic used for default date values in expense entry forms with a call to getTodayIST() from dateRange.ts. Ensure the auto-toll date logic also uses IST so the last toll date comparison is accurate.
Replace any current date derivation logic used for the default date field with a call to getTodayIST() from dateRange.ts to ensure the form defaults to the correct IST date.

Replace any current date derivation logic used for default date range values with calls to getTodayIST() from dateRange.ts to ensure the picker defaults to the correct IST date range when no range is selected.
Replace any current date derivation logic used for the default date fields with calls to getTodayIST() from dateRange.ts to ensure all three expense entries default to the correct IST date.
Replace any current date derivation logic used for the default date field with a call to getTodayIST() from dateRange.ts to ensure the form defaults to the correct IST date when adding new entries.
Replace any current date derivation or comparison logic with calls to getTodayIST() from dateRange.ts to ensure the auto-toll last date tracking and comparison uses IST consistently.
Replace any current date derivation logic in the auto-toll expense logic with a call to getTodayIST() from dateRange.ts to ensure the automatic toll entry uses the correct IST date.
